p0_blocking.title: DESIGN-001: MapboxMapView Wrapper Component

must: Preserve all MapViewWrapper API surface from components/map/map-view.tsx
Convert coordinates from Google [lat, lng] to Mapbox [lng, lat] format
Support camera positioning, markers, and polylines
never: Hardcode map style URLs — use semantic.theme.mapStyleURL
Break existing MapViewWrapper API — this is a drop-in replacement
strictly: Coordinate conversion MUST happen at render boundary, not in storage
All touch targets ≥ 44x44pt for WCAG AA compliance
Screen reader labels on all interactive elements

specification.objective: Create MapboxMapView wrapper as drop-in replacement for Google Maps MapViewWrapper
specification.success_state: All existing map rendering works with Mapbox SDK, theme switching works, camera controls function

acceptance_criteria.then: Mapbox map displays with dark-v11 style URL

acceptance_criteria.when: Camera coordinates and zoom are set
acceptance_criteria.then: Map camera animates to specified position

acceptance_criteria.given: MapboxMapView receives markers in Google [lat, lng] format
acceptance_criteria.then: Markers display at correct Mapbox [lng, lat] coordinates

acceptance_criteria.given: Theme switches from dark to light
acceptance_criteria.then: Map style URL changes to streets-v12

test_criteria.statement: Mapbox map renders with correct dark theme style URL when component mounts with dark theme
Map camera animates to specified position when camera coordinates and zoom are set
Markers display at correct Mapbox coordinates when converted from Google lat/lng format
Map style URL changes to light theme when user toggles theme preference from dark to light

write_prohibited: components/map/map-view.tsx - preserve existing Google Maps implementation
app.json - Expo plugin config requires separate task

references: components/map/map-view.tsx:1-159 - Existing MapViewWrapper API to preserve
interaction_notes: Theme toggle must not flash or flicker
Coordinate conversion must be transparent to callers
design.pattern: export const MapViewWrapper = forwardRef<MapViewHandle, MapViewProps> => const { semantic, dark } = useSemanticTheme()
design.anti_pattern: Direct coordinate storage in Mapbox format — always store in Google format, convert at render

checks: forwardRef for component with imperative handle

p0_blocking.assignment_rationale: Requires React Native + Expo expertise with Mapbox SDK integration

p0_blocking.title: DESIGN-002: CoordinateConverter Utilities

never: Mutate input arrays — always return new arrays
Store coordinates in Mapbox format — storage is Google-agnostic
Throw on invalid input — return null/undefined gracefully
strictly: All functions MUST be pure (same input → same output)
Unit test coverage 100% for edge cases

specification.objective: Create bidirectional coordinate conversion utilities between Google and Mapbox formats
specification.success_state: All coordinate conversions work correctly with full type safety and test coverage

acceptance_criteria.then: All coordinate pairs are swapped
Returns null without throwing

test_criteria.statement: Coordinate swaps lat/lng order when converting from Google to Mapbox format
Coordinate swaps lng/lat order when converting from Mapbox to Google format
Polyline array converts all coordinate pairs when converting array from Google to Mapbox
Null input returns null without throwing when conversion function receives invalid input

write_prohibited: Any storage layer modifications — conversion is render-time only

interaction_notes: No UI interactions — pure utility functions
Used by MapboxMapView and RouteMiniMap components
design.pattern: export const googleToMapbox = (coord: GoogleCoordinate): MapboxCoordinate => ({lng: coord.lng, lat: coord.lat})
design.anti_pattern: Modifying input arrays — always create new arrays with map()

p0_blocking.assignment_rationale: Requires React Native + TypeScript expertise with utility function patterns

p0_blocking.title: DESIGN-003: RouteMiniMap Migration to Mapbox

must: Preserve exact same API surface — drop-in replacement
never: Change component props API — must be backward compatible
Enable map interaction — this is a read-only thumbnail
strictly: All touch targets ≥ 44x44pt (though this component has none)
Screen reader labels on map container

specification.objective: Migrate RouteMiniMap component from Google Maps to Mapbox SDK
specification.success_state: RouteMiniMap renders route preview with Mapbox, all existing usage works unchanged

acceptance_criteria.then: Mapbox map displays route polyline correctly

acceptance_criteria.given: Route geometry has coordinates in Google format
acceptance_criteria.then: Coordinates converted to Mapbox [lng, lat] format

acceptance_criteria.given: Map fits to bounds with padding
acceptance_criteria.then: Route is centered with 1.3x padding multiplier
Tap passes through to parent (pointerEvents='none')

test_criteria.statement: Mapbox map displays route polyline when RouteMiniMap receives overviewGeometry and bounds
Polyline coordinates converted to Mapbox format when route geometry has Google format coordinates
Route centered with padding when map fits to bounds with 1.3x multiplier
Touch events pass through to parent when user taps on mini-map with pointerEvents='none'

write_prohibited: components/chat/routing-card.tsx - consuming component, no changes needed
Any API surface changes — must be backward compatible

interaction_notes: Camera fits bounds with 1.3x padding multiplier
design.anti_pattern: Enabling scrollEnabled/zoomEnabled — this is a static thumbnail

p0_blocking.assignment_rationale: Requires React Native + Expo expertise with Mapbox SDK migration

p1_high.title: DESIGN-004: ShadowSetupWelcome Screen

must: Use semantic theme tokens only (no hardcoded colors)
Display feature highlights with copper accent
Support both light and dark themes
never: Skip screen reader labels on interactive elements
Use pixel values — use semantic.space.* tokens
strictly: Screen reader labels on all buttons

specification.objective: Create welcome screen for Shadow Setup onboarding flow
specification.success_state: Welcome screen displays with feature highlights, CTA button navigates to WiFi check

acceptance_criteria.given: User launches app for first time
acceptance_criteria.then: Feature highlights display with copper accent
Navigation to WiFi check screen
Background uses semantic.color.surface.default
Label reads 'Begin Setup, button'

test_criteria.statement: Welcome screen displays with feature highlights when user launches app for first time
Navigation to WiFi check screen occurs when user taps Begin Setup button
Background color uses semantic surface token when screen renders in dark theme
Screen reader announces button label correctly when focus is on Begin Setup button

write_prohibited: app.json - navigation config requires separate task

interaction_notes: 'Begin Setup' button navigates to WiFi check screen
Feature highlights: Offline routing, Local AI, Cost savings
Gradient copper styling on header text
design.pattern: <SubpageLayout title='Welcome to LaneShadow'><View style={{flex: 1, padding: semantic.space.xl}}>
design.anti_pattern: Hardcoded spacing values — use semantic.space.* tokens

p1_high.assignment_rationale: Requires React Native + Expo expertise with onboarding flow implementation

p1_high.title: DESIGN-005: ShadowSetupWiFi Screen

must: Disable continue button until WiFi connected
Show download size (~800MB) and estimated time (2-5 min)
never: Allow download over cellular — hard requirement
Show continue button as enabled without WiFi
Hardcode download size — fetch from model metadata
strictly: Screen reader announces WiFi state changes
Continue button has disabled state with accessibilityLabel

specification.objective: Create WiFi requirement check screen for model download
specification.success_state: WiFi state detected accurately, continue button disabled until WiFi connected

acceptance_criteria.given: User navigates to WiFi check screen
acceptance_criteria.then: Continue button disabled with 'WiFi Required' message
Continue button enabled with 'Download Your Shadow' label
Show 'WiFi Required — Cellular downloads blocked' message

acceptance_criteria.given: Download size fetched from model metadata
acceptance_criteria.then: Display '~800MB' and '2-5 min' estimated time

test_criteria.statement: Continue button is disabled when user navigates to WiFi check screen without WiFi connection
Continue button enables when user connects to WiFi network
Warning message displays when user has cellular connection only instead of WiFi
Download size and estimated time display when screen renders with model metadata

write_prohibited: Direct NetInfo usage — must use hook
Hardcoded download size — fetch from Convex or model metadata

interaction_notes: Continue button disabled until WiFi detected
design.pattern: const netInfo = useNetInfo(); const isWifi = netInfo.type === 'wifi' && netInfo.isConnected
design.anti_pattern: Allowing cellular downloads — this is a hard security requirement

p1_high.assignment_rationale: Requires React Native + Expo expertise with NetInfo integration

p2_medium.title: DESIGN-006: OfflineRegionDownload Screen

must: Support interactive bounding box with resize handles
Show region size estimate before download
strictly: Screen reader labels on map controls
Contrast ratio ≥ 3:1 for map overlay UI

specification.objective: Create offline region download screen with map-based selection
specification.success_state: User can select region on map, see size estimate, and initiate download

acceptance_criteria.given: User opens offline region download screen
acceptance_criteria.then: Interactive bounding box displays with resize handles
Region size estimate updates in real-time
Warning dialog shows before download initiates

acceptance_criteria.given: User names region and confirms download
acceptance_criteria.then: Progress screen displays with circular indicator

test_criteria.statement: Interactive bounding box with resize handles displays when user opens offline region download screen
Region size estimate updates in real-time when user adjusts bounding box with resize handles
Storage warning dialog displays when user taps download with insufficient storage
Navigation to progress screen occurs when user names region and confirms download

write_prohibited: Mapbox offlineManager direct usage — must use OfflineManager wrapper

interaction_notes: Resize handles on bounding box corners
Size estimate updates as box resizes
Confirmation dialog with region name input
design.pattern: const offlineManager = Mapbox.offlineManager; const pack = offlineManager.createPack({name, styleURL})
design.anti_pattern: Skipping storage validation — always check before download

p2_medium.assignment_rationale: Requires React Native + Expo expertise with Mapbox offline SDK
